Politics.headline: Netanyahu's biggest rivals join forces for Israel's next election
Politics.detail: Two of Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu's most formidable political rivals, Naftali Bennett and Yair Lapid, announced a merger of their parties in a bid to oust his coalition government in the upcoming election expected later this year.

Politics.headline: Trump says Iran can phone if it wants to talk, as Iranian envoy returns to Pakistan
Politics.detail: U.S. President Donald Trump stated that Iran could call to negotiate an end to their two-month war, while Iran's foreign minister returned to Pakistan for talks despite the absence of U.S. counterparts. Trump emphasized that Tehran can never have a nuclear weapon.

Politics.headline: Syrian court begins proceedings against Assad and allies
Politics.detail: A Syrian court conducted the first hearing in the trial of ousted ruler Bashar al-Assad and senior figures from his government, with one of the defendants appearing in person. Assad and his brother Maher have fled Syria and will be tried in absentia.

Politics.headline: Asean News Headlines at 10pm on Sunday (April 26, 2026)
Politics.detail: Asean officials convened to address the Middle Asia crisis' impact on energy supplies. Malaysian Foreign Minister Mohamad is set to lead Malaysia's delegation to the 25th Asean-EU Ministerial Meeting.

Politics.headline: White House Correspondents' Dinner attack suspect is tutor and computer programmer from California
Politics.detail: Social media posts that appear to match the California man arrested Saturday in the shooting at the White House correspondents' dinner show he is a highly educated tutor and amateur video game developer. The suspect in a shooting incident at Saturday’s correspondents’ dinner attended by President Donald Trump and other senior U.S. officials was carrying guns and knives and was believed to be acting alone.

Finance & Economy.headline: Iran war already taking a toll on the global economy, IMF says in new forecast
Finance & Economy.detail: The International Monetary Fund (IMF) has cut its 2026 growth estimate due to the ongoing Iran war, citing an unusual amount of uncertainty clouding the outlook.

Finance & Economy.headline: Syrian-Algerian talks focus on stronger financial and banking cooperation
Finance & Economy.detail: The Governor of the Central Bank of Syria, Abdulkader Husrieh, and the Algerian Ambassador to Damascus, Abdelkader Qasmi al-Hassani, discussed ways to strengthen financial and banking cooperation between the two countries, focusing on expanding cooperation and exchanging expertise to support economic stability.

Finance & Economy.headline: PBOC Conducts RMB400 Billion MLF Operation Today
Finance & Economy.detail: The People's Bank of China (PBOC) conducted a RMB400 billion Medium-term Lending Facility (MLF) operation with a tenor of one year to maintain ample liquidity in the banking system.

Finance & Economy.headline: Home Ministry Urges Local Administrations to Adopt Creative Financing Strategies
Finance & Economy.detail: The Ministry of Home Affairs in Jakarta urged local administrations to implement creative financing strategies to maintain regional development amid global economic uncertainty and fiscal constraints, suggesting maximizing regional assets, CSR funds, and Public-Private Partnerships.

Finance & Economy.headline: State divestment from Viglacera to be delayed
Finance & Economy.detail: The Ministry of Construction in Vietnam will postpone plans to divest state capital from Viglacera, a leading construction material producer and industrial real estate developer, due to valuation challenges and the need to safeguard state assets.

Science.headline: Blood Vessels Found in T. Rex Bones Are Rewriting Dinosaur Science
Science.detail: Researchers discovered a network of preserved blood vessels inside fossilized bones of a Tyrannosaurus rex nicknamed Scotty, challenging previous understandings of dinosaur physiology.

Science.headline: Scientists Uncover How Graphene Oxide Kills Bacteria Without Harming Human Cells
Science.detail: Graphene oxide targets a molecule found only in bacterial membranes, destroying harmful bacteria while leaving human cells unharmed.

Science.headline: Fish Oil May Be Hurting Your Brain, New Study Finds
Science.detail: New research suggests that EPA, a key omega-3 fatty acid in fish oil, may interfere with recovery in people with repeated mild head injuries.

Science.headline: Trump vows to investigate UFO scientist deaths —but experts warn it's a trap
Science.detail: President Donald Trump has promised to look into the mysterious deaths of officials and scientists studying UFOs, but experts suggest this could be a conspiracy theory.

Science.headline: Scientists Identify Protein Behind Ageing Joints In Arthritis Breakthrough
Science.detail: A new study has identified a protein that contributes to aging joints, marking a potential breakthrough in arthritis research.

Health.headline: Fish Oil May Be Hurting Your Brain, New Study Finds
Health.detail: New research suggests that in people with repeated mild head injuries, a key omega-3 fatty acid in fish oil—EPA—may actually interfere with brain repair. Fish oil has long been praised as brain-boosting, but this study suggests the story may be more complicated.

Health.headline: Graphene Kills Harmful Bacteria “Superbugs” but Spares Human Cells
Health.detail: Scientists have uncovered how graphene oxide hunts down and destroys harmful bacteria while leaving human cells completely unharmed. By targeting a molecule found only in bacterial membranes, this ultra-thin material offers a selective approach to combating bacterial infections.

Health.headline: Why are people pumping their bodies with fat from corpses?
Health.detail: An article discusses the trend of using fat from corpses in cosmetic procedures and the ethical considerations surrounding it. The piece explores the implications and potential risks associated with this practice.

Health.headline: Suicide-related callouts to fire services triple in England in a decade
Health.detail: The number of suicide-related callouts to fire services in England has tripled in the last decade, raising concerns about mental health and the increasing role of emergency services in responding to mental health crises. This increase highlights the growing need for mental health support and prevention efforts.

Health.headline: Continued Insulin Pump Use in the Hospital Improves Outcomes
Health.detail: Medscape Medical News reported on April 25, 2026, that continuing insulin pump use in hospitals leads to improved patient outcomes. This finding supports the practice of allowing patients to maintain their insulin pump therapy during hospital stays.

Sports.headline: Sawe takes marathon record
Sports.detail: Kenya's Sabastian Sawe became the first man to run a marathon in under two hours, winning the London Marathon in 1 hour, 59 minutes, 30 seconds, shattering the previous world record. Tigst Assefa of Ethiopia won the women's race, beating her own record set last year in London.

Sports.headline: Hsieh advances in Madrid
Sports.detail: Taiwan's Hsieh Su-wei and her American partner Sofia Kenin advanced to the second round of the women's doubles at the Madrid Open after defeating Tereza Mihalikova and Olivia Nicholls in straight sets. However, Wu Fang-hsien and Eri Hozumi were eliminated in their first-round match.

Sports.headline: Badminton world body changes scoring
Sports.detail: The Badminton World Federation (BWF) voted to adopt a new 15-point scoring system at its annual general meeting in Horsens, Denmark, which will take effect in January of next year. Under the new system, players will need only 15 points to win a game in a 3x15 format.

Sports.headline: Broncos' massive boost; Storm set for mass changes as Eels star escapes ban
Sports.detail: The Brisbane Broncos are set to welcome back two star players, while the Melbourne Storm are expected to make significant changes after their sixth consecutive loss.  Tallyn Da Silva of the Eels avoided suspension but received a fine for a lifting tackle.

Sports.headline: Real Madrid celebrated its sixth League title
Sports.detail: In 1958, Real Madrid secured its sixth League title after drawing against Atlético de Madrid. Rial's equalizing goal in the 76th minute clinched the championship, with Di Stéfano finishing as the top scorer.

Entertainment.headline: 'Michael' moonwalks to US$97 million opening, shattering record for music biopics
Entertainment.detail: The big-budget Michael Jackson spectacle, 'Michael,' shrugged off bad reviews and a troubled production to launch with US$97 million in U.S. and Canada theaters, according to studio estimates Sunday, shattering a record debut for music biopics.

Entertainment.headline: Deepika Padukone, Ranveer Singh announce second pregnancy
Entertainment.detail: Bollywood stars Deepika Padukone and Ranveer Singh have announced that they are expecting their second child.

Entertainment.headline: Jimmy Kimmel on Trump: 'A delicate snowflake with the thinnest fat skin of any human being ever'
Entertainment.detail: Jimmy Kimmel delivered jokes about Donald Trump at the White House Correspondents' Dinner. Seth Meyers also made jokes about Kash Patel.

Entertainment.headline: 'Bhooth Bangla' Day 10: Akshay's film narrowly misses Rs 180 cr mark
Entertainment.detail: Akshay Kumar's horror-comedy 'Bhooth Bangla' is nearing the Rs 180 crore worldwide mark on its tenth day, earning ₹113.40 crore net in India and ₹44.50 crore overseas. This marks a strong comeback for the Priyadarshan-Akshay Kumar collaboration.

Entertainment.headline: Harry Styles engagement speculation grows after girlfriend seen with ring
Entertainment.detail: Speculation about Harry Styles being engaged has increased after his girlfriend was spotted wearing a ring.

Crime & Justice.headline: Suspect in custody after Trump evacuated in shooting incident at White House correspondents' dinner
Crime & Justice.detail: The Secret Service says the US president and first lady are safe and a suspect is in custody after a shooting incident in DC that led to Trump's evacuation from the White House Correspondents' Dinner.

Crime & Justice.headline: White House journalists' gala ended like many US events do: with gun violence
Crime & Justice.detail: The White House Correspondents' Dinner was interrupted by gunfire, highlighting the ongoing issue of gun violence in the United States.

Crime & Justice.headline: D.C. shooting suspect aired grievances against Trump in writings to family
Crime & Justice.detail: Investigators are examining the writings, social media posts, and family interviews of the suspect in the D.C. shooting as evidence of the suspect's mindset and motivations.

Crime & Justice.headline: 5 injured in shooting near Indiana University following Little 500 races
Crime & Justice.detail: A shooting near Indiana University, after the Little 500 races, has left five people injured.

Crime & Justice.headline: Singer D4vd charged with murder of 14-year-old girl
Crime & Justice.detail: Singer D4vd faces first-degree murder and additional charges that could lead to life without parole or the death penalty.

Education.headline: Goa SSC Result 2026 Released: 94.51% Candidates Pass Class 10 Board Exams
Education.detail: The Goa Board of Secondary and Higher Secondary Education (GBSHSE) announced the SSC results for 2026, with an overall pass percentage of 94.51%. Students can access their results on the official website.

Education.headline: Andhra Pradesh SSC Results 2026: Know How To Access Your Marks On WhatsApp
Education.detail: Students in Andhra Pradesh can now check their SSC results for 2026 through WhatsApp, providing a convenient and accessible way to view their scores. The Board of Secondary Education, Andhra Pradesh (BSEAP) has facilitated this service.

Education.headline: Physical education must be reformed
Education.detail: National Taiwan Normal University (NTNU) suspended an associate professor and terminated a coach's contract after an investigation into alleged abuses of authority and bullying of students, highlighting structural issues in athletic training cultures where students have little space to refuse due to power imbalances.

Education.headline: NEET UG 2026 Admit Cards Out; Exam Scheduled For May 3
Education.detail: Admit cards for the National Eligibility cum Entrance Test (NEET) UG 2026 are now available, with the examination scheduled for May 3. Candidates can download their admit cards from the official website.

Education.headline: Polson Family Update: April 26, 2026
Education.detail: Polson Park Public School shared updates including upcoming events such as a Grade 6 Girls' Basketball game, Subway Lunch Delivery, and Neurovibes Event, as well as information about the Polson Track and Field Day on May 1st.

Lifestyle.headline: National Walk Safely to School Day is on Friday 22 May 2026
Lifestyle.detail: Primary school-aged children across Australia will step towards a healthier future next month on National Walk Safely to School Day, Friday 22 May 2026, to encourage a more active lifestyle and reduce car dependency. The initiative highlights the health, safety, transport and environmental benefits of walking or using active travel to get to and from school every day.

Lifestyle.headline: Singaporean influencer injured foot after pull-up, triggering gout
Lifestyle.detail: Singaporean influencer Kelvin Tan, also known as Mayiduo, shared that he experienced foot pain after attempting a pull-up, which led to a diagnosis of severe gout. He reminded everyone to buy health insurance, as his hospital stay cost more than S$4,000, but was covered by insurance.

Lifestyle.headline: Inside the world of ultra-luxury wedding cakes
Lifestyle.detail: French pastry chef Bastien Blanc-Tailleur creates ultra-luxury wedding cakes, with custom-made cakes starting at 20,000 euros ($23,500) and elaborate signature pieces costing multiple times that. One project comprised five cakes in total, took an estimated 3,500 hours of work, and was for the wedding of billionaire heiress Netra Mantena and tech entrepreneur Vamsi Gadiraju.

Lifestyle.headline: Word of the Day for April 27, 2026: kadaugan
Lifestyle.detail: A paralyzed lovebird parrot was able to fly again after being placed on a drone to tour a park in Ciudades, Davao City. The 10-month-old lovebird, Nitya, is placed inside a plastic bottle attached to the drone with holes for ventilation, making it part of Nitya's daily entertainment.

Lifestyle.headline: Missing lion cub is reunited with family after rescue.
Lifestyle.detail: The Washington Post reports on the heartwarming reunion of a missing lion cub with its family following a successful rescue operation.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Editorial | Robots are all well and good – so long as they do no harm to humans
Opinion/Editorial.detail: An editorial emphasizes the importance of ensuring robots do not harm humans as their use becomes more prevalent.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Editorial | Malaysia bourse pact extends Hong Kong's outreach to Southeast Asia
Opinion/Editorial.detail: An editorial discusses the significance of the pact between the Malaysian and Hong Kong stock exchanges for extending Hong Kong's influence in Southeast Asia.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Li Xing. Opinion | 122 years on, Mackinder's 'heartland' remains geopolitically decisive
Opinion/Editorial.detail: An opinion piece reflects on how Mackinder's 'heartland' theory continues to hold geopolitical relevance 122 years later.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: World Bank, IMF must redefine strategy to close poverty gap
Opinion/Editorial.detail: An editorial argues that the World Bank and IMF need to change their strategies to effectively address the poverty gap, including offering more flexible financing and incorporating the voices of emerging economies.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: The Guardian view on Trump and the Washington shooting: political violence and gun culture endanger all
Opinion/Editorial.detail: The Guardian's editorial board discusses the dangers of political violence and gun culture after a shooting at the White House Correspondents' Dinner.

World.headline: Global military spending surges on insecurity: report
World.detail: Global military spending reached nearly $2.9 trillion in 2025, marking an 11th consecutive year of growth, as insecurity and rearmament fuelled defence budgets, with the United States, China and Russia being the top spenders. Spending rose by 2.9 percent compared with 2024, despite a reduction by the US, the world's biggest spender.

World.headline: Mali Defence Minister killed in attack that saw jihadist and rebels seize towns, military bases
World.detail: Militants and separatists launched coordinated attacks across Mali, resulting in the death of the Defence Minister Sadio Camara. The attacks also led to the seizure of towns and military bases.

World.headline: Israel launches deadly strikes in Lebanon, warns residents beyond 'buffer zone' to flee
World.detail: Israeli airstrikes in Lebanon resulted in multiple fatalities, prompting warnings for residents beyond a designated 'buffer zone' to evacuate. The Israeli government and Hezbollah have traded blame over breaches to the truce, which is set to run for several more weeks.

World.headline: Magnitude 6.2 quake shakes Japan's Hokkaido island
World.detail: A strong earthquake with a magnitude of 6.2 rattled Japan's northern island of Hokkaido.  The quake struck at a depth of 83 kilometers, and no tsunami alert was issued.

World.headline: At least 19 killed, dozens injured after explosives detonated in Colombia highway attack
World.detail: Explosives detonated on a highway in Colombia, resulting in at least 19 deaths and numerous injuries. The incident is under investigation as authorities work to determine the cause and identify those responsible.

Zimbabwe.headline: Zimbabwe to scrap trade barriers for regional countries
Zimbabwe.detail: Zimbabwe is set to eliminate trade barriers for regional countries in an effort to boost trade. The move is expected to stimulate economic growth and create new opportunities for businesses in the region.

Zimbabwe.headline: Speaker Mudenda sounds the alarm on soaring imports
Zimbabwe.detail: Speaker of Parliament, Jacob Mudenda, has expressed concern over the increasing levels of imports into Zimbabwe. He warned that this trend could negatively impact the country's economy and called for measures to promote local production.

Zimbabwe.headline: Integrity, selflessness key to leadership: VP Chiwenga
Zimbabwe.detail: Vice President Constantino Chiwenga emphasized the importance of integrity and selflessness in leadership positions. He urged leaders to prioritize the needs of the people and serve with dedication and honesty.

Zimbabwe.headline: Zim's crisis of fake medicines, goods
Zimbabwe.detail: Zimbabwe is facing a crisis involving counterfeit medicines and goods, raising concerns about public health and safety. Authorities are working to combat the issue and protect consumers from harmful products.

Zimbabwe.headline: Chiwenga vows new era of policy consistency and reforms
Zimbabwe.detail: Vice President Chiwenga has pledged a new era of policy consistency and reforms in Zimbabwe, aiming to create a more stable and predictable economic environment. The government is committed to attracting investment and promoting sustainable growth.
